expectations.text: Report contains a Summary section with a brief overview of the codebase and key findings
expectations.evidence: Lines 9-11: Summary describes the project and gives a count of findings by severity category.

expectations.text: Report contains a structured Findings section with individual issues
expectations.evidence: Lines 17-207: 36 findings organized by severity tiers.

expectations.text: Each finding has a severity level (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
expectations.evidence: Uses non-standard severity groupings: CRITICAL, HIGH (for security), MODERATE, PERFORMANCE, LOW. 'Performance' and 'Security' are used as severity levels instead of categories. Does not use the standard Critical/High/Medium/Low scale.

expectations.text: Each finding has a category (Correctness/Security/Error Handling/Performance/Maintainability)
expectations.evidence: No explicit category labels on individual findings. Findings are grouped by severity tier, with security and performance used as both severity and implicit category.

expectations.text: Each finding cites specific file paths with line numbers
expectations.evidence: Most findings include file paths and line numbers, e.g., 'project_constellation/main.py (line 10)'.

expectations.text: Each finding includes evidence such as code snippets or specific patterns
expectations.evidence: Most findings include code snippets showing the problematic code, though some lower-severity items have brief descriptions only.

expectations.text: Each finding includes a suggested fix
expectations.evidence: Most findings include a 'Fix:' line, though format is inconsistent and some are brief.

expectations.text: Each finding includes an effort estimate (Small/Medium/Large/Architectural)
expectations.evidence: No effort estimates are provided for any finding.

expectations.text: Findings are ordered from highest to lowest severity
expectations.evidence: Findings are grouped: CRITICAL → HIGH (Security) → MODERATE → PERFORMANCE → LOW.

expectations.text: Report includes an Issues Not Found section noting categories with no significant findings
expectations.evidence: No 'Issues Not Found' section. Report instead has a 'Prioritized Remediation Plan' table and 'Architectural Observations' section.

expectations.text: No findings are speculative -- every issue is backed by specific code evidence
expectations.evidence: S3 finding says 'the pattern of inline SQL encourages future SQL injection vulnerabilities' -- this is speculative since the query has no user input. Also L2 about AssertionError has contradictory text similar to the with-skill version.
